text.title: AI Workload Cost Attribution Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when ai-workload-cost-attribution is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), a root cause (`reason`), and a corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: Per-tenant cost numbers swing 30% month-over-month with no obvious cause.
antipattern.reason: Pricing catalogue version not pinned; provider quietly changed unit prices.
antipattern.note: Fix: Pin catalogue version per report; on price change, regenerate prior month for diffability.

antipattern.description: Finance refuses to use the report; says it doesn't match the invoice.
antipattern.reason: Reconciliation step skipped; tax + reservations + commit credits ignored.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add reconciliation step; surface variance as a numbered line item.

antipattern.description: `UNATTRIBUTED` bucket grows quietly to 40% of cost.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add the 2% threshold + alarm; force a root-cause for every breach.
